refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple obligations and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Establish real-time communication quality monitoring with feedback control to dynamically optimize network usage
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and protocol parameters
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be implemented independently from other system functions. It avoids coupling with specific hardware or technologies and supports modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately and timely
Risk that adaptation may not fully compensate for sudden network degradation
Assumes customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including error detection and correction as a complementary DP to enhance robustness, or clarify if it is subsumed under the adaptive protocol approach.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to monitor network performance metrics and send/receive data packets

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network connection for data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion since protocol adjustments are localized here.
Low containment; vehicle module focuses on physical and control aspects, not network protocol adaptation, causing high diffusion if contained here.
Moderate containment; teleoperation depends on communication but protocol adaptation is more network-centric, leading to moderate diffusion.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety logic, not communication protocol, causing high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
If network link interface changes, adaptation logic may require updates, impacting modularity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the adaptive communication protocol is well localized within this module. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) provide necessary data to monitor packet loss and adjust parameters, enabling testing against the acceptance criterion of packet loss ≤ 1%.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, as it is responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation, aligning well with the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document the dependency on telemetry data and define interface contracts to minimize coupling and maintain modularity.
Plan for interface versioning or abstraction layers to mitigate impact of potential changes in the Network Link interface on the adaptation logic.
Include verification steps in the test plan to measure packet loss under agreed network conditions to ensure compliance with acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The adaptive communication protocol is well localized within the NGHE Solution module, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Although minor coupling risks exist, they are manageable through recommended revisions without necessitating further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and modularity at this level is preferable to avoid complexity and potential integration risks.

revise.why: To explicitly document the dependency on telemetry data and define interface contracts to minimize coupling and maintain modularity, as well as plan for interface versioning or abstraction layers to mitigate impact of potential changes.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol sufficiently meets the FR acceptance criteria for packet loss ≤ 1%.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementing the DP.
Verification methods and test plans will be developed to measure packet loss under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules causing minor coupling.
Possible impact of changes in the Network Link interface on adaptation logic.
evidence_needed: Verification test results demonstrating packet loss ≤ 1% under operational conditions.
Interface contracts and versioning plans to manage dependencies and modularity.
